content.problemStatement:
**Sliding Window Maximum**

You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers and an integer XXX.

Find the **maximum** element in each subarray of size XXX.

Print (N−X+1)(N-X+1)(N−X+1) space-separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the maximum element of the ithi^{th}ith subarray of size XXX from left.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of elements in the array and the size of subarrays.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** element in each subarray of size XXX.

Print (N−X+1)(N-X+1)(N−X+1) space-separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the maximum element of the ithi^{th}ith subarray of size XXX from left.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 200000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and X — the length of the array and the window size. The next line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N‑X+1 space‑separated integers. The i‑th integer is the maximum of the subarray A[i] … A[i+X‑1] (1‑based indexing).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: windows of size 2 → maxima are 2,3,4. Test case 2: windows of size 3 → maxima are 4,3. Test case 3: window size 1 → each element is its own maximum.
First test: size‑3 windows give maxima 5,5,4. Second test: size‑2 windows give maxima 2,3.
Sliding a window of length 5 over the increasing sequence yields the last element of each window as the maximum.

solution.approach: Maintain a deque that stores indices of elements in decreasing order of their values. For each new element i, remove indices that are out of the current window (i - X). Then pop from the back while the current element is larger than the element at those indices, ensuring the deque front always holds the index of the maximum for the current window. After processing i >= X-1, output the value at the deque front. This yields O(N) time per test case and O(X) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(X) for the deque, at most the window size.
